no worries at all.

Yeah.

you know, um, something about this year is, it's wedding year, um, between my family and friends.
So it's just been, I mean, nonstop.

A lot of weddings, a lot of stuff going on.
So, that makes sense.

Yes.
I'm based in Nashville.
Awesome.
Awesome.
And that's where corporate is at.
SNS, right?
Ok.
Ok.
Cool.
Awesome.

I know.

Um and see if it's uh a good fit over there at S MS.
Um So before we start, I do have a question.
So, um I feel like I've gotten emails from three different people at rentable
and I'm really confused kind of on the structure.
So Shelby Buchanan
has been like our account executive.
Um And then someone else, a Jason reached out to me.
OK, about um but it says business development rentable and it, he was asking specifically about rentable Google Business Pro.
So I'm just confused because stuff gets like thrown on my calendar and I'm not really sure what the structure is.
No, that's uh it's a good question.
Um I can definitely see how that's uh confusing with the outreach here.

Um So recently, rentable is kind of structured in the three different departments now where um like you mentioned, Shelby uh handles Google Business Pro and specifically that product which is automating and optimizing your Google business profiles on the property level.

Um And then there's an apartment IQ department um which it looks like you've met with relatively recently, um which concentrates on data collection and making sure that your units are priced correctly and so forth.
And then our original product, which is just our, our ils and uh the loose um we have rentable in our affiliate network and then we do targeted social ads for lead gen on both Facebook and Instagram.
Um So that's kind of the three pronged approach.
Um I'm not sure and I know it's super confusing and Alyssa and, and I think Patrick emailed you to start, um, and you jumped on this call.
Now, is this sort of what you were expecting to discuss today, the, the lead generation and advertising side of things or did you just kind of hop on to sort that out?
Well, it was kind of confused because I've had three people from principle, um reach out to me recently.
So quite honestly, I got lost in what we were covering.
Uh but it's not, it's not business prep.
Correct.
No.
So I wouldn't.
Well, that's,

that's perfect because I had responded last week saying I wasn't interested in business pro just because we have, we have another product that basically pushes out any updates um across, not just Google Business, but, you know, our Apple Maps.
Yelp, all of that.

I appreciate you.

Absolutely.

Um, and then I'll show you what we can do to potentially, um, give you more leads, boost occupancy for any properties that could be falling behind or that could use a little boost here, um, throughout the remainder of the year, I'll get you some pricing and, and get you on out of here.
Does that sound good to you?
Sounds good?
Ok, perfect.
Um So yeah, I guess real, real quickly um would love to just hear like a, a brief overview on on your portfolio.
Um number of properties potentially what you're currently doing for advertising and lead gen and what's been working and not working.
Um That'd be, that'd be fantastic.
Yeah, so um I have been with S MS since October of last year.
So we're like 9, 10 months.
Um and I guess it was the end of September, but we have at the moment about 53 properties.
Um We have a couple of properties that are transitioning out of tax credit.
Um One is actively doing so and one is about to um you know, we are looking at growth and acquisitions within the company, which is great.
Um When I started, we were on one main ils platform basically.
Um And then we did have rentable live um on all of our properties and then we did, I have diversified spend quite a bit.

And ultimately why we cut rentable is because rentable has been great for certain properties, but we had all the properties signed up on it and that cost was having to be allocated to properties that were virtually getting, you know, like I think if I remember correctly off the top of my head, maybe even less than five leads a month and it kind of went back and forth with Shelby and really wasn't able to reach an agreement on um you know what that pricing structure looked like because I was going to have to still, if we kept rentable live, I would still have to allocate more to properties where I wasn't, it was kind of pushing my cost per lead threshold.
Um because the, the certain properties that performed very well on it,
you know, I'm fine with like the cost that we were allocating.
But once we removed the properties that were not performing well and had that new pricing structure, I just don't think it was working very well.
Um And additionally, we didn't have super high like conversion.
So I will to kind of give you a little bit of extra context to our teams have not been using any lead management platform.
So when I started, I really struggled.

So I started at the end of the year um and then had to ultimately just go in and do property budgets, but I didn't have all of the information because I noticed a lot of our teams were not entering in traffic into one site.
So since then, we have rolled out AC RM on a handful of properties that we're testing with the goal of rolling that out to the rest of the portfolio by the end of this year.
And that is where kind of my like digital advertising strategy will change looking at next year because I'll be able to truly track
our conversions and the lead source.

even though the le leads were higher.
